subtitleChunks.text: So the core thing with Supabase
is you get a full PostgreSQL
database for every project, not some
weird proprietary thing, actual PostgreSQL. But
they bolt on real time functionality,
backups, extensions, the whole deal. So
you're not just getting a database,
you're getting a database that can
push changes to your front end
the second data changes. That's the
main sell. You keep all the
PostgreSQL knowledge you already have, all
the SQL you already know, and
you just get a bunch of
extra stuff on top. If you've
ever set up PostgreSQL, a REST
API, a auth, and file storage
separately, this is all of that
in one dashboard. That's basically it.
